2026-04-13T09:05:37.0222020Z changes and commit them, and you can discard any commits you make in this
2026-04-13T09:05:37.0222748Z state without impacting any branches by switching back to a branch.
2026-04-13T09:05:37.0223213Z 
2026-04-13T09:05:37.0223510Z If you want to create a new branch to retain commits you create, you may
2026-04-13T09:05:37.0224207Z do so (now or later) by using -c with the switch command. Example:
2026-04-13T09:05:37.0224616Z 
2026-04-13T09:05:37.0224764Z   git switch -c <new-branch-name>
2026-04-13T09:05:37.0225031Z 
2026-04-13T09:05:37.0225164Z Or undo this operation with:
2026-04-13T09:05:37.0225404Z 
2026-04-13T09:05:37.0225531Z   git switch -

      },
      "event": {
        "type": "string",
        "enum": [
          "APPROVE",
          "REQUEST_CHANGES",
          "COMMENT"
        ]
      }
    }
  }
}

2026-04-13T09:05:53.3149705Z ##[endgroup]

2026-04-13T09:13:21.1425898Z 
2026-04-13T09:13:21.1426402Z ◆ I found that `delete` is used as a method name in multiple operations:
2026-04-13T09:13:21.1427165Z   - `CommunicationServicesOperations.delete` (line 156)
2026-04-13T09:13:21.1427783Z   - `DomainsOperations.delete` (line 297)
2026-04-13T09:13:21.1428324Z   - `EmailServicesOperations.delete` (line 374)
2026-04-13T09:13:21.1428922Z   - `SenderUsernamesOperations.delete` (line 679)
2026-04-13T09:13:21.1429834Z   - `SmtpUsernamesOperations.delete` (line 739)
2026-04-13T09:13:21.1430769Z   - `SuppressionListAddressesOperations.delete` (line 763)
2026-04-13T09:13:21.1431478Z   - `SuppressionListsOperations.delete` (line 823)
2026-04-13T09:13:21.1432009Z   
2026-04-13T09:13:21.1432301Z   
2026-04-13T09:13:21.1433774Z   - `delete` is a reserved keyword in JavaScript/TypeScript, but it can be used as a property name when accessed via dot notation, similar to how `Map.prototype.delete()` works, so this shouldn't actually be a problem.
2026-04-13T09:13:21.1435397Z   
2026-04-13T09:13:21.1442677Z   However, I'm noticing that previous PR reviews flagged `delete` as an issue in mgmt-review guidelines, particularly in PR #37954 where `DependencyOfRelationshipsOperations.delete` was called out. But looking at the actual codebase, `delete` has been consistently used across mgmt SDKs, and the pattern of having both `beginDelete`/`beginDeleteAndWait` alongside `delete` methods appears standard in TypeSpec-generated SDKs. Let me check some known-good TypeSpec-migrated packages to see if this naming convention is actually acceptable. I'm realizing that while `delete` can technically work as a property name in TypeScript, the mgmt-review guidelines seem to flag it anyway based on the precedent from PR #37954, even though this pattern appears consistently across TypeSpec-generated ARM SDKs. Looking at the guidelines, there's a specific note about not flagging `AzureClouds` enums since the inconsistency is intentional. The `delete` keyword issue seems to be a separate known problem, so I should check the management review guidelines file to see if there's more context on how to handle this.
2026-04-13T09:13:21.1449776Z 
2026-04-13T09:13:21.1450199Z ✓ glob(pattern: **/*mgmt-review*guidelines*, path: /home/runner/work/azure-sdk-for-js/azure...)

If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Comments will be on the RIGHT side of the diff.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Review comment content in Markdown. Provide specific, actionable feedback about the code at this location.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"line":{"description":"Line number for the comment. For single-line comments, this is the target line. For multi-line comments, this is the ending line.","type":["number","string"]},"path":{"description":"File path relative to the repository root (e.g., 'src/auth/login.js').
